title: podman machine: share environment variables between WSL and windows
body:
### Feature request description

Please add support for podman machine to share environment variables between WSL and windows , according to following [microsoft devblog article](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/commandline/share-environment-vars-between-wsl-and-windows/).

Currently windows environment variables exposed via _WSLENV_ environment variable, are not exposed to podman machine instance(s).

comments.body: This seems to be a bug, related to #15967 breaking wsl interoperability. When enabling native systemd support in /etc/wsl.conf windows environment variables are passed into default podman machine instance. Unfortunately this breaks 'podman machine ssh' access.

Yes, I confirm that this is a bug (assuming that we expect users to use a podman machine as a proper WSL distro).

You can still:
```
wsl -d <podman-machine-name>
$ exit
$ echo $WSLENV
WT_SESSION:WT_PROFILE_ID:
```

@baude @mheon we may want to consider https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15967 for next quarter
ok. lets change it to a feature request and that will get it looped in.
I am interested in investigating this issue and proposing a fix. However, is my understanding of the conversation above correct, that this work is blocked by #15967 / requires extra planning?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> I am interested in investigating this issue and proposing a fix. However, is my understanding of the conversation above correct, that this work is blocked by [#15967](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15967) / requires extra planning?

@Samathingamajig my apologies for not answering earlier. Yes this issue will be addressed once #15967 is fixed. And I plan to work on that next month. I don't think it's worth fixing this issue as a standalone issue.
